input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

visible_c_inputs.question: Which American economist, winner of the 1976 Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences, wrote the introduction for the 1996 reprint of "I, Pencil"?
passages: I, Pencil | "I, Pencil" is an essay by Leonard Read.  The full title is "I, Pencil: My Family Tree as Told to Leonard E. Read" and it was first published in the December 1958 issue of "The Freeman".  It was reprinted in "The Freeman" in May 1996 and as a pamphlet entitled "I.. .  Pencil" in May 1998.  In the reprint, Milton Friedman wrote the introduction and Donald J. Boudreaux wrote the afterword.  Friedman (the 1976 winner of the Nobel Prize in Economics) used the essay in his 1980 PBS television show "Free to Choose" and the accompanying book of the same name.  In the 2008 50th Anniversary Edition, the introduction is written by Lawrence W. Reed and Friedman wrote the afterword.

Roger Myerson | Roger Bruce Myerson (born March 29, 1951) is an American economist and winner of the Sveriges Riksbank Prize in Economic Sciences in Memory of Alfred Nobel, sometimes referred to as the "Nobel Prize" for economics, with Leonid Hurwicz and Eric Maskin for "having laid the foundations of mechanism design theory."  A professor at the University of Chicago, he has made contributions as an economist and as a political scientist.
Michael Spence (disambiguation) | Michael Spence is an American-born economist and winner of the 2001 Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences

James Mirrlees | Sir James Alexander Mirrlees {'1': ", '2': ", '3': 'FRSE FBA', '4': "} (born 5 July 1936) is a Scottish economist and winner of the 1996 Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences.  He was knighted in 1998.
Edmund Phelps | Edmund Strother Phelps, (born July 26, 1933) is an American economist and the winner of the 2006 Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences.
John Harsanyi | John Charles Harsanyi (Hungarian: "Harsányi János Károly" ; May 29, 1920 – August 9, 2000) was a Hungarian-American economist and Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences winner.

Robert F. Engle | Robert Fry Engle III (born November 10, 1942) is an American economist and the winner of the 2003 Sveriges Riksbank Prize in Economic Sciences in Memory of Alfred Nobel, sharing the award with Clive Granger, "for methods of analyzing economic time series with time-varying volatility (ARCH)".

retrieved_titles: Milton Friedman Institute for Research in Economics
Enhanced Primary Care Case Management Program

w_plus_state.output: The first-hop evidence (Medical facilities of Seattle) identifies Group Health Cooperative as “one of the pioneers of managed care in the United States.” Bridge entity: Group Health Cooperative. Missing fact needed for the final answer: the year Group Health Cooperative was established/founded. Retrieval direction for the next hop: retrieve the “Group Health Cooperative” page (look in the article lede, infobox, or history/organization sections for founding/establishment/incorporation year; also check reliable sources cited there).
w_plus_state.generated_query: Group Health Cooperative founded year site:wikipedia.org OR "Group Health Cooperative history" site:grouphealth.org OR "Group Health Cooperative founded"
retrieved_titles: National Association for Public Health Policy

delta_p.trigger: If any input passage contains the substring "pioneer" or "pioneers" (case-insensitive) AND the question requests a year/founding/establishment, apply this delta.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep extracting and citing the supporting passage/title and preserve the exact entity token(s) as they appear in the passages (e.g., 'Group Health Cooperative'). Keep concise, factual language summarizing what the first-hop passage states.
delta_p.change_rule: Do not attempt to produce a final numeric answer. Instead, produce a concise first-hop summary that: (1) names the supporting passage/title or quotes the short phrase from it (use at most a short quoted phrase already present in the passages, e.g. "one of the pioneers of managed care"); (2) explicitly identifies the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages; (3) states the single missing fact needed for the next hop (the founding/establishment year); and (4) gives a clear next-hop retrieval direction: retrieve the bridge entity's page and check the lede/infobox or history/organization sections for the founding/establishment year. Do not include the founding year or other facts not present in the given passages.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Avoid outputting any numeric year, any invented facts, long verbatim spans that are not already visible in the passages, or step-by-step query strings. Avoid turning the summary into a final answer; avoid adding unrelated retrieval suggestions.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce only the 'summary' text. The summary must be 1–3 short sentences (or a 2–4 line bullet-style list) containing: supporting source/title or a short quote present in the passages, the bridge entity name verbatim, the missing fact needed (phrased as 'founding/establishment year'), and the precise next-hop direction (retrieve the bridge entity page; check lede/infobox/history). Do not include a numeric year or extra speculative details.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The agent currently outputs a final numeric year instead of exposing the bridge entity and the missing fact/direction needed for the next hop. This delta enforces the agent role (summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge/missing fact) so the pipeline proceeds to the correct retrieval.

delta_p.trigger: If any passage mentions 'Group Health Cooperative' or contains wording like 'pioneers of managed care'.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the agent's existing behavior of extracting and concisely summarizing the first-hop evidence from the provided passages (cite the passage title or fragment that supports the claim).
delta_p.change_rule: Also explicitly (a) name the bridge entity found in the first-hop evidence (e.g., 'Group Health Cooperative'), (b) state the specific missing fact needed for the next hop in descriptive terms (the founding/establishment/incorporation year), and (c) give a precise next-hop retrieval direction: retrieve the 'Group Health Cooperative' article/page and check the article lede, infobox, and history/organization sections (and the reliable sources cited there) for the founding/establishment year. Do not attempt to supply the year here.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not state or guess the founding year, do not provide a final answer to the question, and do not output external search queries or fetch results yourself. Avoid adding unrelated background or long extraneous details—only include what is needed to enable the next hop.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce the field `summary` that (1) cites the passage evidence that identifies Group Health Cooperative as a pioneer of managed care, (2) explicitly names 'Group Health Cooperative' as the bridge entity, (3) identifies the missing fact as the organization's founding/establishment year (worded, not a numeric year), and (4) gives the clear next-hop retrieval direction described above. Keep the summary concise and do not include the founding year or any final answer.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The current summary must be augmented to expose the bridge entity and to give a concrete retrieval direction for finding the establishment year. This small, local edit prevents premature answering and ensures the next agent knows exactly where to look for the missing founding/establishment date.

match.transported_damage_hypothesis: When the 'I, Pencil' page (first hop) does not explicitly show the 1996 reprint introduction author or the Nobel-year qualifier, the summary1 agent will fail to expose the bridge entity (the introduction author) and will omit concrete next-hop retrieval guidance; instead it will emit a terse final answer (the economist's name) or otherwise present the answer without documenting the missing evidence.
match.expected_damaged_behavior: At the summary1 boundary the agent returns a one-line answer naming the economist without (a) listing the first-hop evidence that is missing the intro-author fact, (b) labeling the bridge entity/page that must be fetched next (the author’s biography or the 1996 reprint section), or (c) issuing explicit retrieval instructions for the next hop.
match.rationale: The W sample (w_index 97) corrects a failure where the agent should have exposed a bridge entity and requested the bridge page (the organization) but instead produced a premature final answer (a founding year). The same local failure pattern — failing to surface the required bridge (the intro author) and failing to request a follow-up retrieval, while producing a terse final answer — maps directly to the target question: answering who wrote the 1996 reprint introduction requires exposing the missing author fact and issuing a next-hop retrieval for that author or the reprint details. Transporting this bridge-relation omission / premature-answer pattern is semantically plausible and local to the summary1 agent boundary without needing global changes.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are constructing a target-specific transported damaged output C_minus
for one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Inputs:
- C_plus is a correct current output/state for the target sample.
- W_minus -> W_plus is a matched W-side repair transition.
- The ordered W delta-p trace describes the repair behavior.

Task:
Infer the reverse W failure pattern and apply only that failure pattern to
the target sample's own visible inputs, producing a plausible LOCAL neighbor
C_minus of C_plus.

Strict rules:
- Produce an actual output for the selected agent boundary, not a prompt.
- Preserve the agent's native input/output contract.
- Use only visible_c_inputs as factual content.
- Never copy W-side entities, answers, titles, or sample-specific literals
  unless they independently occur in visible_c_inputs.
- Do not create unrelated corruption. Preserve enough useful C context for
  the result to remain a local neighbor.
- Damage one coherent behavior: weaken, remove, or blur an anchor, relation,
  qualifier, candidate distinction, evidence integration, output selection,
  or another matched failure pattern.
- An answer flip is not required and must not be claimed.
- Do not mention gold labels, oracle targets, evaluation metrics, source or
  target states, transport, prompt updates, or optimization in damaged_output.
- Do not wrap damaged_output in explanatory prose.
